description_long: This house features a modern, angular design with two main wings connected by a central entryway. The exterior is finished in a combination of muted green and beige wall panels, complemented by large, rectangular windows that allow ample natural light. The roof is predominantly gabled, with dark, matte shingles that contrast with the lighter wall colors. The front entry is defined by a covered walkway and a paved path leading up to the door, providing a welcoming approach. The window arrangement is asymmetrical, with some sections featuring floor-to-ceiling glass and others with smaller, more traditional windows. The overall structure is low and wide, suggesting a single-story layout with a focus on horizontal space.
description: A modern, single-story house with a gabled roof, large asymmetrical windows, and a central covered entryway connected by a paved path.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, covered walkway, and large windows on both wings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, uninterrupted wall with three evenly spaced windows and the extension of the paved path.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back facades of both wings, with large windows and a clear view of the roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing additional windows and the connection between the two main wings.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large picture windows
attribute_entryway_design: Central covered entryway with a paved path, flat overhang, and large glass panels flanking the door
